LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAMON PADILLA'S MOTION FOR PAYMENT OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND REIMBURSMENT OF EXPENSES - THE COURT HAS CAREFULLY REVIEWED EACH CHALLENGED TIME ENTRY AND MADE REDUCTIONS TOTALING $3,726.50 FOR TIME THE COURT CONCLUDED WAS UNNECESSARY, INSUFFICIENTLY SUPPORTED, OR NOT PROPERLY COMPENSATED. THE COURT DECLINES TO AWARD A MULTIPLIER IN LIGHT OF THE BILLING RATE REQUESTED THAT ADEQUATELY COMPENSATES FOR UNCERTAINTY AND DELAY FACTORS INHERENT IN THIS KIND OF LITIGATION. THE COURT OTHERWISE DETERMINES THAT THE BILLING RATE AND TIME SPENT ARE REASONABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE, AND THE REQUESTED COSTS AND EXPENSES ARE PROPERLY ORDERED REIMBURSED. THE TOTAL FEES AND COSTS TO BE PAID BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF ARE $49,204.50. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 23, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 4PM MAY 16 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE. IF THE COURTESY COPIES ARE NOT RECEIVED BY THAT DATE, THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. THE COURT HAS RECEIVED COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
NOTICE OF RULING FILED BY DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES USA, INC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. NEITHER OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ACCRUE FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURPOSES UNTIL PLAINTIFF DISCOVERED OR REASONABLY SHOULD HAVE DISCOVERED THE ALLEGED BREACH OF WARRANTY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHEN PLAINTIFF DISCOVERED OR REASONABLY SHOULD HAVE DISCOVERED THE ALLEGED BREACH OF WARRANTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursment Of Expensers
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LARRY W. CHAE OF THE BICKEL LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (619) 374-4100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JASON T. WELCH OF BEATTY & MYERS, LLP, TEL. NO. (562) 606-1530 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES. USA INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RAMON PADILLA'S Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursment Of Expenses - The Court has carefully reviewed each challenged time entry and made reductions totaling $3,726.50 for time the Court concluded was unnecessary, insufficiently supported, or not properly compensated. The Court declines to award a multiplier in light of the billing rate requested that adequately compensates for uncertainty and delay factors inherent in this kind of litigation. The Court otherwise determines that the billing rate and time spent are reasonable under the circumstances of this case, and the requested costs and expenses are properly ordered reimbursed. The total fees and costs to be paid by Defendant to Plaintiff are $49,204.50.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/PJB

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursment Of Expensers

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maija Olivia, Esq., from Beatty & Myers, LLP, 562-606-1524, for Defendant and moving party
Erika Kavicky, Esq., from The Bickel Law Firm, Inc., 619-374-4100, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Neither of plaintiff's claims accrue for statute of limitations purposes until plaintiff discovered or reasonably should have discovered the alleged breach of warranty. There is a triable issue when plaintiff discovered or reasonably should have discovered the alleged breach of warranty. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-15-2012 Continued To May-23-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTOR SALES U.S.A., INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Continued to May 23, 2012 to provide defendant with the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant must deliver courtesy copies of its moving papers to department 302 no later than 4pm May 16 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. If the courtesy copies are not received by that date, the motion will be denied. The court has received courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
